input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Need to fix the benchmark config for multi-TFM. BenchmarkDotNet handles this differently.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet's multi-TFM support works differently - it needs to be run from one framework and it will spawn the other. Let me run it with --framework net10.0 (the latest SDK) and let BenchmarkDotNet handle both jobs. But wait, the jobs I configured don't specify different runtimes - they're just default jobs. I need to configure them with specific runtimes.

Let me fix the benchmark to use proper multi-TFM BenchmarkDotNet configuration.

toolCalls.name: edit
bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-c988cec604fc438289c814140e3cbefb/Benchmarks && dotnet run -c Release --framework net10.0 2>&1", "description": "Run benchmarks with BenchmarkDotNet", "initial_wait": 600}

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-c988cec604fc438289c814140e3cbefb/Benchmarks && grep -r "Net10\|Net100\|Net90\|Net9_0" ~/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet/0.14.0/ --include="*.cs" -l 2>/dev/null; dotnet build -c Release --framework net10.0 2>&1 | grep -i "runtimemoniker" | head -5; python3 -c "
import zipfile, re, sys, glob
